free_text: Gamma-widths and strength functions in text.
Neutron capture cross section measurements of 155Gd
to produce pulsed neutrons between 1.1 to 220 keV.
Electrons were impinging on a water-cooled
tantalum target to produce neutrons by photonuclear
Enriched isotopes of Gd-155 and Gd-157 were obtained
in the form of oxide powders.The chemical impurities
in both isotopes were negligible.The thicknesses of
the samples were 0.791*10**-3 atoms/barn for Gd-155
placed at 54.45m from the neutron source,were attached
to a polyethylene disk (5 % boron-loaded) to
moderate the neutrons and were exposed to the neutron
beam at the center of the capture gamma ray detector.
path up to 500 eV and up to 2 keV respectively for
measured the neutron flux and was located 1.89m
Used for the prompt capture gamma rays.
The n-source strength was monitored by a Li-6 glass
Al27(N,A) reaction is not mentioned in the articles as
monitor. But main reference is "private communication
with author" - so monitor information is not corrected
.The results are corrected for the effects of the
resonance self shielding and the multiple scattering
which were found to be less than 1 %.
Free text was corrected to be human-readable.
page short abstract,no data) was deleted.
.The capture cross  sections have been determined from
the detection of the prompt capture gamma rays and
normalized by the saturated resonance technique.
The capture cross-sections have been deduced from the
TOF spectra and normalized by the saturated resonance
The average resonance parameters were obtained by the
statistical model analysis of the capture cross
Cross sections were fitted in the region 1.1-100 keV
by the least squares method using code SALS.
